courses.subject: CENG
courses.title: Digital Logic Design Laboratory
courses.description: This course aims at providing an intensive hands-on introduction to digital system design using a hardware description language (e.g. VHDL). Students will learn how to design, simulate and debug digital systems through lab exercises. Topics include the basic language syntax, signals/variables/constants declaration, data types, basic operators, concurrent and sequential statements, and structural modelings.
courses.course_syllabus: This course aims at providing an intensive hands-on introduction to digital system design using a hardware description language (e.g. VHDL). Students will learn how to design, simulate and debug digital systems through lab exercises. Topics include the basic language syntax, signals/variables/constants declaration, data types, basic operators, concurrent and sequential statements, and structural modelings.
courses.recommended_readings:
1. Digital Design with RTL Design, VHDL, and Verilog, 2/e, by Frank Vahid;
2. VHDL for Digital Design, by Frank Vahid & Roman Lysecky

courses.subject: CENG
courses.title: Fundamentals of Embedded Systems
courses.description: This course provides foundations on essential elements in design and development of embedded systems. Topics include 3D engineering drawings, lab instrumentation, electronic circuit analysis, digital signal processing, microcontroller interfacing, sensors, and actuators. Students can gain hands-on experience from laboratory works and projects.
courses.learning_outcomes:
At the end of the course of studies, students will have acquired the ability to
1. Draw 3D engineering drawings using SolidWorks
2. Solve and analyze electronic circuits
3. Understand the basic principles of digital signal processing
4. Develop basic microcontroller-based embedded systems
courses.course_syllabus:
Week 1: 3D Drawing Using SolidWorks
Week 2: 3D Parts Assembling Using SolidWorks
Week 3: Lab Instrumentation
Week 4: Circuit Analysis
Week 5: Op-amp Circuits
Week 6: Digital Signal Processing I – Frequency Analysis
Week 7: Digital Signal Processing II – Sampling, ADC & DAC
Week 8: Project 1 Week 1
Week 9: Project 1 Week 2
Week 10: Microcontroller Interfacing
Week 11: Sensors and Actuators
Week 12: Project 2 Week 1
Week 13: Project 2 Week 2
courses.feedback_evaluation:
1. Quiz and examinations
2. Course evaluation and questionnaire
3. Question-and-answer sessions during class
4. Student consultation during office hours or online
courses.recommended_readings:
1. James W. Nilsson, Susan A. Riedel, “Electric Circuits”, Upper Saddle River, N.J. : Prentice Hall
2. McClellan, James H., “Signal Processing First”, Upper Saddle River, N.J. : Prentice Hall
3. Evans, Brian, “Beginning Arduino Programming”, Berkeley, CA : Apress

courses.subject: CENG
courses.title: Embedded System Design

meetings.location: William M W Mong Eng Bldg 404

courses.description: This course introduces the techniques for building embedded systems such as the use of microprocessors, interfacing memory systems and timing control of digital signals. It also discusses peripheral input/output interfacing methods such as: timer, serial and parallel interfaces, analog-to-digital conversion, polling, interrupt and device driver developments. Assembly language programming will also be introduced: concepts of addressing modes, data manipulation, control flow instructions, programme linkage and relation to high level languages.
courses.enrollment_requirement:
Prerequisite: ENGG2020 or ENGG2120 or ESTR2104.
Not for students who have taken ESTR2100.
courses.course_syllabus: This course introduces the techniques for building embedded systems such as the use of microprocessors, interfacing memory systems and timing control of digital signals. It also discusses peripheral input/output interfacing methods such as: timer, serial and parallel interfaces, analog-to-digital conversion, polling, interrupt and device driver developments. Assembly language programming will also be introduced that includes: concepts of addressing modes, data manipulation, control flow instructions, programme linkage and relation to high level languages.
courses.recommended_readings:
1. Furber, ARM System-on-Chip Architecture, 2000;
2. Web-based data sheets and teaching materials;

courses.subject: CENG
courses.title: Computer Organization and Design

meetings.location: William M W Mong Eng Bldg LT
William M W Mong Eng Bldg LT
William M W Mong Eng Bldg LT

courses.description: This course introduces the organization and design of modem computer systems. The scope includes instruction set architecture, performance evaluation, design of arithmetic logic units, datapath and control, pipelining, memory hierarchy, interfacing processors and peripherals, and multi-core processors.
courses.enrollment_requirement:
Not for students who have taken CSCI3420.
Prerequisite: ENGG2020 or ENGG2120 or ESTR2104.
For 2nd-year entrants, the prerequisite will be waived.
courses.course_syllabus: This course introduces the organization and design of modem computer systems. The scope includes instruction set architecture, performance evaluation, design of arithmetic logic units, datapath and control, pipelining, memory hierarchy, interfacing processors and peripherals, and multi-core processors.
courses.required_readings: 1. Patterson and Hennessy, Computer Organisation & Design, The Hardware/Software Interface, 3rd edition

courses.subject: CENG
courses.title: Rapid Prototyping of Digital Systems

meetings.location: William M W Mong Eng Bldg 404
William M W Mong Eng Bldg 404
William M W Mong Eng Bldg 404

courses.description: This course introduces digital prototyping techniques such as the use of a hardware description language for hardware system development, and the methods for interfacing field programmable devices to microprocessors, memory systems, and peripheral devices.
courses.enrollment_requirement:
Prerequisite: CSCI2510 or ENGG2020 or ENGG2120 or ESTR2104 or its equivalent
For senior-year entrants, the prerequisite will be waived.
Not for students who have taken ESTR3100
courses.course_syllabus: This course introduces digital prototyping techniques such as the use of a hardware description language for hardware system development, and the methods for interfacing field programmable devices to microprocessors, memory systems, and peripheral devices.
courses.feedback_evaluation:
1. Mid-term course and teaching evaluation;
2. Final course and teaching evaluation;
courses.required_readings:
1. Digital Design Principles and Practices by John F. Wakerly The fourth edition, Prentice Hall;
2. Advanced Signal Integrity for High-Speed Digital Design, Stephen H. Hall and Howard L. Heck , Wiley;

courses.subject: CENG
courses.title: Computer-aided Design for Very Large Scale Integrated Circuits

meetings.location: William M W Mong Eng Bldg 804
William M W Mong Eng Bldg 804
William M W Mong Eng Bldg 804
William M W Mong Eng Bldg 804
William M W Mong Eng Bldg 804
William M W Mong Eng Bldg 804

courses.description: This course aims at providing students basic knowledge and background on VLSI CAD. The course will cover various topics on automation across the design of a VLSI circuit including but not limited to, Boolean matching, logic optimization, chip planning, placement, routing, interconnect optimization, testing and manufacturing. Students will learn to use some EDA tools and also develop some algorithms to solve EDA problems.
courses.enrollment_requirement: Prerequisite: CSCI2100 or CSCI2520 or ESTR2102
courses.learning_outcomes:
At the end of the course of studies, students will have acquired the ability to
1. Use some EDA tools from vendors like Cadence and Synopsys.
2. Understand the objectives and constraints in different stages of an EDA flow.
3. Understand the basic methodologies and algorithms to solve problems in different stages of an EDA flow.
4. Innovate and develop some new methods in solving EDA problems.
5. Know and appreciate the new developments in the EDA industry.
courses.course_syllabus: This course aims at providing students basic knowledge and background on VLSI CAD. The course will cover various topics on automation across the design of a VLSI circuit including but not limited to, Boolean matching, logic optimization, chip planning, placement, routing, interconnect optimization, testing and manufacturing. Students will learn to use some EDA tools and also develop some algorithms to solve EDA problems.
courses.feedback_evaluation:
1. Quiz and examinations
2. Course evaluation and questionnaire
3. Question-and-answer sessions during class
4. Student consultation during office hours or online
courses.recommended_readings:
1. “VLSI Physical Design: From Graph Partitioning to Timing Closure” by A.B. Kahng, J. Lienig, I.L. Markov and J. Hu. (Publisher: Springer)
2. “Practical Problems in VLSI Physical Design Automation” by Sung Kyu Lim, (Publisher: Springer)
3. “VHDL for Logic Synthesis”, Andrew Rushton, 3rd. Ed. (Publisher: Wiley)
4. "CMOS VLSI Design - A Circuits and Systems Perspective" by Neil H.E., Weste and D. Harris 3rd. Ed. (Publisher: Addison-Wesley)

courses.subject: CENG
courses.title: Embedded System Development and Applications
courses.description: This course aims at enhancing students’ skills in developing embedded systems. Advanced techniques in the use of real-time operating systems, interfacing, integration and applications of sensors and actuators for signal analysis and control will be discussed. Project development methods and management skills will be introduced.
courses.enrollment_requirement:
Not for students who have taken CENG3480 in 2008-09 and before;Prerequisite: CENG2400 or ESTR2100.
For 2nd-year entrants, the prerequisite will be waived.
courses.learning_outcomes:
1. Students should be able to mange an embedded system development project.
2. Students should understand the difficulties of sensing signals from sensors and control actuators in an embedded system and be able to address them.
3. Students should understand the skills required to design a reliable embedded system.
courses.course_syllabus: This course aims at enhancing students’ skills in developing embedded systems. Advanced techniques in the use of real-time operating systems, interfacing, integration and applications of sensors and actuators for signal analysis and control will be discussed. Project development methods and management skills will be introduced.
courses.feedback_evaluation:
1. Course evaluation
2. Feedback though discussions in laboratory classes.
courses.recommended_readings:
1. MicroC OS II: The Real Time Kernel (With CD-ROM) by Jean J. Labrosse
2. High-Speed Digital Design: A Handbook of Black Magic by Howard W. Johnson and Martin Graham Prentice Hall

courses.subject: CENG
courses.title: Final Year Project I
courses.description: The course is designed to provide students with an opportunity to carry out, under the supervision of an academic staff, an independent project with research elements in engineering.
courses.learning_outcomes:
1) Be able to identify and have a basic understanding of the literature related to the project topic.
2) Be able to define and complete a project that utilizes results in the literature.
3) Be able to perform a critical review of the project.
4) Develop technical writing skills.
courses.course_syllabus: The course includes, but are not limited to, the following elements: literature review, modelling, system design and implementation, project evaluation.
courses.required_readings: Literature review materials will be recommended by the project supervisor.
